title: urtica urens subsp. oblongata
intro: Urtica urens subsp. oblongata, also known as dwarf stinging nettle, is a subspecies of stinging nettle, a perennial plant that belongs to the nettle family (Urticaceae). It is characterized by its ability to cause skin irritation and blistering upon contact, due to the presence of urtic acid in its leaves and stems. The medicinal properties of Urtica urens subsp. oblongata include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, which are obtained through the extraction of its leaves and roots. Herbal preparations of Urtica urens subsp. oblongata, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, gout, and urinary tract infections, and are valued for their potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system.
